flashcards.front: What is the primary purpose of Use Case diagrams in UML?
flashcards.back: To visually represent system functionality from an end-user perspective and model interactions between actors and system functionalities.
An external entity (user or system) interacting with the system being designed.
Mandatory dependency where one use case incorporates the behavior of another.
Extend represents optional/conditional behavior while Include is mandatory.

flashcards.front: What is a System Boundary in Use Case diagrams?
flashcards.back: Rectangle separating internal system components from external actors.

flashcards.front: Name three elements of Use Case diagrams.
flashcards.back: Actors, Use Cases, Relationships, System Boundary.

flashcards.front: What is Generalization in Use Cases?
flashcards.back: Inheritance relationship where child use case inherits parent's behavior.

flashcards.front: Why are Use Cases critical in requirement analysis?
flashcards.back: They bridge communication gaps between stakeholders and developers.

flashcards.front: What is the main limitation of Use Case diagrams?
flashcards.back: They don't show sequence or implementation details.

flashcards.front: Give an example of a primary vs secondary actor.
flashcards.back: Primary: Bank customer. Secondary: Credit bureau system.
